description: Leber hereditary optic neuropathy (LHON) primary-mutation panel: MT-ND4 m.11778G>A (rs199476112; ref G, risk A; ~70% of cases, poor visual recovery), MT-ND1 m.3460G>A (rs199476118; ref G, risk A; ~15%, poor recovery), MT-ND6 m.14484T>C (rs199476104; ref T, risk C; ~15%, best visual prognosis). Together these three primary mtDNA mutations explain >90% of LHON; all are ClinVar Pathogenic complex-I variants. Honesty guardrails (§12.5/§12.6): mtDNA is maternally inherited and these are typically homoplasmic, but arrays give a single binary call and cannot measure heteroplasmy; penetrance is incomplete and sex-biased (~50% of male carriers, ~10% of female carriers ever lose vision, often triggered by smoking/alcohol) so a positive call is never a diagnosis or prediction; a negative/absent call never rules LHON out (rare/private mutations and untyped positions exist). These positions are frequently off-chip (off_chip_risk=high) -> absent probe surfaced as indeterminate, never a false-negative. Not nuclear ClinVar P/LP in the engine's sense -> clinvar_significance=NULL. EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §9 / roadmap #50.

genotype_models.risk_classification: MT-ND4 m.11778G>A — primary LHON mutation detected
genotype_models.finding_text: A primary LHON mutation, MT-ND4 m.11778G>A (rs199476112 {genotype}), was detected. This is the most common LHON variant (~70% of cases) and is associated with poor spontaneous visual recovery. Importantly, most carriers never lose vision: penetrance is incomplete and sex-biased — roughly half of male carriers and about one in ten female carriers ever develop optic neuropathy, often triggered by environmental factors such as smoking or heavy alcohol use. A positive result is not a diagnosis and not a prediction that you will lose vision.

genotype_models.risk_classification: MT-ND1 m.3460G>A — primary LHON mutation detected
genotype_models.finding_text: A primary LHON mutation, MT-ND1 m.3460G>A (rs199476118 {genotype}), was detected. This accounts for roughly 15% of LHON cases and is associated with poor spontaneous visual recovery. Most carriers never lose vision: penetrance is incomplete and sex-biased — about half of male carriers and one in ten female carriers ever develop optic neuropathy, often triggered by smoking or heavy alcohol use. A positive result is not a diagnosis and not a prediction.

genotype_models.risk_classification: MT-ND6 m.14484T>C — primary LHON mutation detected
genotype_models.finding_text: A primary LHON mutation, MT-ND6 m.14484T>C (rs199476104 {genotype}), was detected. This accounts for roughly 15% of LHON cases and carries the best visual prognosis of the three primary mutations, with spontaneous recovery seen in some affected individuals. Most carriers never lose vision: penetrance is incomplete and sex-biased — about half of male carriers and one in ten female carriers ever develop optic neuropathy, often triggered by smoking or heavy alcohol use. A positive result is not a diagnosis and not a prediction.
